content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN positive integers.

We have a **weighted undirected** complete graph GGG with NNN vertices numbered 111 to NNN.

The edge between vertices (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) has a weight of min⁡(∣i−j∣,∣Ai−Aj∣)\min(|i-j|,|A_i-A_j|)min(∣i−j∣,∣Ai​−Aj​∣) for all (1≤i<j≤N)(1\le i \lt j \le N)(1≤i<j≤N).

We define the diameter of GGG as max⁡1≤u<v≤nd⁡(u,v)\max\limits_{1 \le u < v \le n}{\operatorname{d}(u, v)}1≤u<v≤nmax​d(u,v), where d⁡(u,v){\operatorname{d}(u, v)}d(u,v) is the length of the **shortest path** between vertex uuu and vertex vvv.

Find the diameter of GGG.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the length of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ representing the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the diameter of graph GGG.
constraints: 2 ≤ N ≤ 2000 (sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2000)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N (2 ≤ N ≤ 2000), the number of elements. The second line contains N space‑separated positive integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the diameter of the graph, i.e. the maximum value of min(|i‑j|, |Ai‑Aj|) over all 1 ≤ i < j ≤ N.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: all A are equal, so |Ai‑Aj|=0 and every edge weight is 0. Diameter = 0.
Test case 2: edge weights are 1,1,2; the maximum is 2.
Test case 3: edge weights are 1,1,0; the maximum is 1.
Value differences are much larger than index differences, so each edge weight equals the index distance. The largest index distance is 3 (between vertices 1 and 4).
Here |Ai‑Aj| equals |i‑j| for every pair, therefore the maximum min is the maximum index distance, which is 5.

hidden.label: maximum boundary (15 elements, value differences huge)

solution.approach: For any two vertices i and j the edge weight is w(i,j)=min(|i‑j|,|Ai‑Aj|). The weight function satisfies the triangle inequality, therefore the direct edge is always the shortest path between i and j. Consequently the diameter equals the maximum edge weight in the complete graph. We simply iterate over all unordered pairs, compute w(i,j) and keep the maximum. With N ≤ 2000 this O(N^2) scan is easily fast enough.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2) per test case (at most about 4·10^6 operations for the whole input).

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        A = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        ans = 0
        for i in range(N):
            ai = A[i]
            for j in range(i+1, N):
                d_idx = j - i
                d_val = abs(ai - A[j])
                w = d_idx if d_idx < d_val else d_val
                if w > ans:
                    ans = w
        out_lines.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
